mechanism_narrative: A sustained high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment, which typically leads to higher discount rates applied to equity cash flows, thus predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (e.g., > 60% positive days) when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a decline in consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are declining while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains high, it indicates that high interest rates are effectively curbing consumer demand, signaling an impending economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both conditions (RSAFS decline and high FEDFUNDS) are met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion indicate robust consumer and business confidence, which typically translates into sustained economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is above 1400.0k units during an expansionary period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns for S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth over a 5-year rolling window (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) below a certain threshold signals that the Federal Reserve may ease monetary policy, leading to lower discount rates and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth falls below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) are growing strongly but retail sales (RSAFS) are stagnant or declining, it suggests a divergence where employment gains are not translating into consumer spending, indicating potential future economic weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS growth is strong but RSAFS growth is weak.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) during a recovery phase indicates robust economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% during a recovery period.
